subject: Geology
title: Plate Sizes and Speeds
description: A micro-lesson that makes Plate Sizes and Speeds usable.

hook.text: The Pacific Plate is the largest on Earth, and it's moving northwest at about seven centimeters per year.

buildup.text: Plates range from massive (Pacific, North American) to small (Juan de Fuca, Cocos). Size varies enormously.

discovery.text: GPS satellites now track plate motion in real time. Speeds range from one to fifteen centimeters per year.

twist.text: That's roughly as fast as your fingernails grow. Slow by human standards, but it reshapes the planet over time.

climax.text: Even small motions create enormous effects. A few centimeters a year becomes thousands of kilometers over eons.

punchline.text: Fingernail speed builds and destroys oceans.

quiz.question: How fast do tectonic plates typically move?
